description_long: This is a large, military-style, twin-fuselage airplane with a dark green camouflage paint scheme and white insignia markings on the wings and fuselages. The aircraft features four propeller engines mounted on a straight, mid-mounted wing that connects the two fuselages. Each fuselage has its own cockpit area, and the tail section consists of two vertical stabilizers connected by a horizontal stabilizer, forming an H-tail configuration. The landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be retractable. The overall design is reminiscent of World War II heavy bombers or reconnaissance aircraft, with a robust and utilitarian appearance.
description: A large, twin-fuselage military airplane with four propeller engines, dark green camouflage, and white insignia. It features an H-tail and mid-mounted straight wings.
description_view_0: Oblique top-down view showing both fuselages, four engines, and the full H-tail configuration.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the straight wingspan, twin fuselages, and the alignment of the engines.
description_view_2: Oblique top-down view from the opposite side, again showing both fuselages, engines, and tail structure.
description_view_3: Frontal view displaying the four propeller engines, twin fuselages, and the cockpit areas.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings connecting twin fuselages, no visible winglets, slight dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Four piston engines, tractor configuration, mounted on the leading edge of the wing.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers joined by a horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable, main gear under each fuselage.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpits at the front of each fuselage, multi-seat configuration.
